goal: Turn /app into the first real relationship cockpit for a returning Orbit user while preserving the existing approved capability behavior.

success_criteria.description: GET /app first viewport foregrounds one top relationship priority with the person or account in focus, why it matters now, readable source context, and the next safe action; the page no longer opens as a generic capability inventory.

Secondary home sections summarize contacts, event opportunities, follow-ups, chat context, dashboard health, and agent review as user outcomes with readable labels and clear links, not as internal modules or service names.

The home page has one obvious primary action for the current next move and clear secondary paths; all visible actions have accessible names, no empty anchors/buttons, and no horizontal overflow at 375px.

Readable source/provenance context is visible near the priority and secondary summaries; raw IDs such as evidence:*, queue:*, source:*, and provider-like labels appear only inside details or hidden technical evidence, never in primary copy.

tests/pages/app-page.test.tsx and product copy tests assert the relationship-cockpit narrative, primary/secondary action hierarchy, source visibility, and internal-vocabulary guardrails; npm test and npm run build pass with no backend, service, API, feature, sample data, or other public route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing API envelopes, route handlers, feature contracts, service factories, or sample data fixtures
Changing app shell global navigation, global CSS, or shared visual-system primitives unless a failing test proves the home page cannot fit
Implementing contact acquisition, contact detail, event, follow-up, chat, dashboard, or agent route polish reserved for Sprints 75-79
